spec_match.rationale: Builds a worklet that filters interestGroups() by owner/name/lookback and contributes per-segment counts — exactly the chromestatus framing — but uses 'lastUpdated' as the field name rather than the spec's 'lookbackWindow', which silently misrepresents the schema visitors will see in real code.

interactive.rationale: Three text fields drive both a live filtered IG list and a regenerated worklet code block; the histogram contributions counter updates in lockstep.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Card promises a builder over mock IGs with live histogram + worklet code update; page delivers exactly that.

edge_cases.rationale: Numeric input is unbounded and accepts non-numbers (would NaN the filter), there's no demo of an empty filtered set producing zero contributions, and the contribution count is a 1:1 with filtered IGs which conflates 'IGs the user is in' with 'segments to contribute to'.

references.rationale: see-also section is missing the chromestatus.com/feature/5074536530444288 link — the page links the sibling concept and the feature index but not the canonical reference required by the project's self-heal rule.

design_system.rationale: All colours via CSS variables, font-display + font-mono used appropriately; no button-background overrides without colour, no static-card layout.

openQuestions.title: Add the chromestatus reference link
openQuestions.detail: The see-also list is missing the https://chromestatus.com/feature/5074536530444288 link. This breaks the project's invariant #3 that every page must carry the chromestatus URL so the /tmp/fix-slugs.py recovery script can find the canonical ID if folder names get garbled.

openQuestions.title: Rename lastUpdated to lookbackWindow
openQuestions.detail: The spec field is lookbackWindow (milliseconds since join/refresh, surfaced on each IG returned from interestGroups()). The mock fixture uses 'lastUpdated' as days, which both misnames the property and uses the wrong unit. Either rename to lookbackWindow with ms values, or keep the friendlier days but call the field 'lookbackDays' and add a note that the real field is ms.

openQuestions.title: Validate the lookback input
openQuestions.detail: The number field accepts free text and the filter does ig.lastUpdated <= +look, so non-numeric input silently filters everything out. Clamp to a numeric range and reflect invalid input in the UI.

openQuestions.title: Distinguish IG-membership count from contribution count
openQuestions.detail: Right now the contributions stat equals the filtered IG count, which suggests 'one contribution per IG'. The real algorithm aggregates by segment bucket — so two IGs in the same bucket contribute once. Add a 'distinct buckets' stat alongside.

summary: Useful builder UI with a real worklet code block that updates live, but it skips the chromestatus reference (breaking invariant #3) and misnames the spec's lookbackWindow field. Fix those, then split bucket count from IG count.
